# Textarea

A multiline text control for messages, notes, and longer free-form values.

## Installation

Add Textarea from the configured StackTrace registry.

```shell
npx shadcn-vue@latest add @stacktrace/textarea
```

## Usage

Use Textarea when line breaks are meaningful or the expected value is too long for a single-line Input. Give it a visible label and a concrete placeholder only when an example helps users understand the expected content.

**Textarea example**

```vue
<template>
  <Textarea placeholder="Type your message here." />
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import { Textarea } from '@/Components/Textarea'
</script>
```

## Controlled and initial values

Bind `v-model` when other interface state needs the current text, such as a preview or character counter. Use `default-value` for initial content that does not need an external source of truth. Textarea forwards native attributes including `name`, `maxlength`, `required`, and `autocomplete`.

**Controlled value example**

```vue
<template>
  <Field class="w-full max-w-sm">
    <FieldLabel for="textarea-bio">Short biography</FieldLabel>
    <Textarea
      id="textarea-bio"
      v-model="biography"
      maxlength="160"
      placeholder="Tell people what you work on."
    />
    <FieldDescription>
      {{ biography.length }}/160 characters
    </FieldDescription>
  </Field>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import { Field, FieldDescription, FieldLabel } from '@/Components/Field'
import { Textarea } from '@/Components/Textarea'
import { ref } from 'vue'

const biography = ref('')
</script>
```

## Sizing

The component starts with a minimum height and uses content-based field sizing. Let it grow for most free-form content, or set a purposeful height or row count when the available space is constrained. Avoid a one-line textarea; use Input when multiline entry is not intended.

## States

Use `disabled` only when the value is unavailable and should not be submitted. Prefer `readonly` when users should still focus, select, or copy the content. Disabled styling alone is not an explanation, so keep the reason visible when it is not obvious.

**Disabled example**

```vue
<template>
  <Textarea disabled placeholder="Type your message here." />
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import { Textarea } from '@/Components/Textarea'
</script>
```

## Validation and accessibility

Put `aria-invalid` on Textarea after validation fails and render the error in a nearby `FieldError`. Use `aria-describedby` for persistent constraints such as a required format or character limit. Do not rely on a counter alone to explain what content is expected.

## Choosing a control

> **Use Input for short single-line values: [Input](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/input)**
>
> Names, email addresses, search terms, and similar short values belong in Input unless line breaks are a valid part of the answer.

> **Use Input Group for an inline counter or action: [Input Group](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/input-group)**
>
> When a compact counter or action belongs inside the textarea boundary, compose Input Group Textarea with a block addon.

Hosted documentation: [Textarea](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/textarea)
